data_in.description: task_001.expected_outputs[0]: methylDiff object containing differentially methylated bases with hyper- and hypo-methylated base counts
methylBase object from unite() on example CpG files

data_out.description: Dendrogram object showing hierarchical clustering of samples
Scree plot showing variance explained by each principal component (PNG or PDF)
PC1/PC2 scatter plot visualization (PNG or PDF)

domain_knowledge: Hierarchical clustering of methylation profiles groups samples by overall methylation similarity, enabling discovery of sample structure and potential batch effects.
Principal component analysis on methylation data reduces dimensionality while preserving variance; PC1 and PC2 typically capture the largest sources of methylation variation across samples.
The scree plot quantifies how much total variance each principal component explains; interpretation guides whether downstream analyses should use one, two, or more PCs.
Dendrogram branch heights reflect distances (typically Euclidean or correlation-based) between sample methylation profiles; shorter distances indicate more similar methylation landscapes.
Visualization of sample clustering is essential quality control to identify outliers, validate expected group separations, and detect confounding factors before differential methylation analysis.

direct_checks: verify that inputs include a united methylBase object (output of unite() on example CpG files) or a concrete file path to such an object in the methylKit package repository
verify that clusterSamples() function is callable on the methylBase object and produces a dendrogram object (class 'dendrogram' or equivalent hierarchical clustering structure)
verify that PCASamples() function is callable on the methylBase object and produces at least two plot files in PNG or PDF format

verify that the dendrogram object can be serialized and matches the visual structure described in the methylKit vignette (no canonical answer for exact tree topology, but structure must be reproducible from the same input)
verify that the PCA scree plot file exists and contains variance explained per principal component
verify that the PC1/PC2 scatter plot file exists and displays sample clustering in 2D space
script_runs: both clusterSamples() and PCASamples() execute without error on the provided methylBase input
file_format_is: output plot files are valid PNG or PDF (robust to file format choice)
expert_review: visual inspection of dendrogram: confirm that hierarchical clustering structure is biologically plausible given the input methylation data (e.g., expected samples cluster together, no inverted relationships)

visual inspection of PCA plots: confirm that the scree plot shows expected variance decay and that PC1/PC2 scatter reflects meaningful sample separation (no canonical answer; requires domain judgment of methylation patterns)
confirm that clusterSamples() and PCASamples() implementations in the methylKit package match the documented behavior in the vignette

The following function will cluster the samples and draw a dendrogram.'

[other] clusterSamples() produces a dendrogram showing sample clustering by correlation distance with Ward linkage, and PCASamples() generates a scree plot and PC1/PC2 scatter plot revealing methylation profile relationships among the four samples.: 'clusterSamples(meth, dist="correlation", method="ward", plot=TRUE)
```

Setting the `plot=FALSE` will return a dendrogram object which can be
manipulated by users or fed in to other user functions'

[methods] No detailed methods section content provided; only vignette header and setup code present.: 'Document contains only vignette header and setup code, no methods section text'

cmd: library(methylKit); mBase <- readRDS('{inputs.methylbase_object}'); clusterSamples(mBase, dist='correlation', method='ward', plot=TRUE); pdf('dendrogram.pdf'); clusterSamples(mBase, dist='correlation', method='ward', plot=TRUE); dev.off(); pca <- PCASamples(mBase, screeplot=TRUE); pdf('screeplot.pdf'); PCASamples(mBase, screeplot=TRUE); dev.off(); pdf('pc1_pc2_scatter.pdf'); PCASamples(mBase); dev.off();

finding: clusterSamples() produces a dendrogram showing sample clustering by correlation distance with Ward linkage, and PCASamples() generates a scree plot and PC1/PC2 scatter plot revealing methylation profile relationships among the four samples.
inputs: task_001.expected_outputs[0]: methylDiff object containing differentially methylated bases with hyper- and hypo-methylated base counts
methylBase object from unite() on example CpG files
methodology_summary: Load the united methylBase object containing merged methylation calls across all samples.
Apply hierarchical clustering via clusterSamples() to compute pairwise sample distances and construct a dendrogram.
Apply principal component analysis via PCASamples() to identify major axes of methylation variation.
Generate scree plot showing cumulative and per-component variance explained by principal components.
Generate PC1 vs. PC2 scatter plot to visualize sample grouping in the first two principal components.
Validation: Dendrogram and PCA plots should visually match the vignette outputs and support expected sample stratification based on methylation profiles.
missing_information: No detailed methods section content provided; only vignette header and setup code present.

task_description: From a united methylBase object, perform unsupervised clustering and dimensionality reduction to obtain a dendrogram and principal component analysis plots (scree plot and PC1/PC2 scatter) visualizing sample relationships.
task_objective: Generate a dendrogram from clusterSamples() and two PNG/PDF plots (scree plot and PC1/PC2 scatter) from PCASamples() using the united methylBase object.
title: Reproduce sample clustering and PCA outputs from the methylBase object

Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: knitr, Scree plot showing variance explained by each principal component (PNG or PDF).
workflow_description: 1. Load the methylBase object produced by unite() from example CpG methylation files. 2. Apply clusterSamples() function to perform hierarchical clustering on methylation profiles and generate a dendrogram object. 3. Apply PCASamples() function to compute principal components and generate a scree plot showing variance explained by each PC. 4. Extract and visualize the first two principal components (PC1 and PC2) as a scatter plot to assess sample grouping and methylation-based similarity.
